text.title: Read-Only Investigation by Default Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for inc-read-only-investigation-default. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Agent RBAC default policy MUST be Deny on all write verbs; allowed writes MUST be explicitly enumerated.

rationale.source: AWS IAM least-privilege + Kubernetes RBAC docs

rule.rationale: AWS IAM least-privilege + Kubernetes RBAC docs — default-allow is the precondition for every prod-incident-from-agent.

rule.statement: Allowed read tooling: log search, dashboard fetch, code search, runbook fetch, alert inspection, PagerDuty status read. Anything not on the allowlist is denied.
rule.rationale: Treat tool allowlist as zero-trust; opt-in not opt-out.

rule.statement: Every write action (rollback, flag toggle, scale, restart, schema migrate) MUST present a signed approval token (`gov-approval-token-signed-jwt`) bound to that action class.
rule.rationale: Approval-token discipline; chain to the JWT approval methodology.

rule.statement: Approval tokens MUST be bound to action classes (e.g. `rollback:payments-service`); a token for `rollback` cannot authorise `restart`.
rule.rationale: Capability-based security — over-scoped tokens are the root of agent escalation incidents.

rule.statement: First successful write of an action class MUST be recorded in the trust ratchet (action class, approver, agent id, evidence); subsequent writes reference the ratchet entry.
rule.rationale: Trust escalation requires audit precedent — silent first-time writes erode the policy floor.

rule.statement: Mid-incident escalation of agent permissions MUST require a separate human approval + audit entry; agent cannot be granted broad permissions in panic.
rule.rationale: Post-incident audits repeatedly find permissions granted in haste and never revoked.

rule.statement: Post-incident, agent permissions MUST be reverted to default Deny set; permission grants are time-bounded.
rule.rationale: Tokens leak into the next incident otherwise.

rule.statement: If agent has no execution surface or team has no incident-response automation, return `skipped`.
